answers.supported_exploration: I went into this with zero knowledge on Baldur's Gate 3, so I did not know anything. I used the circle on the right to understand the abilities of each class/race (e.g., whether they did fire damage or acid damage or how the extent of their utility). I noticed on my second character that there were differences per race, so I tried to make my third and fourth character based on race. This was to maybe fill in the gaps that could be there. So, I basically looked more at the third tab when setting up a character at this point.
answers.blocked_exploration: Whenever I wanted to try out my build it said that the server was occupied. So, I did not get to actually try out many things or see the synergy between my characters. I just used my usual playstyle in games to assess what I would like my party to have.
answers.changed_normal_planning: I think I was more aware of everything. I usually avoid details when I plan a party or build a character. I prefer to just play the party to see what I think about it and if the party achieves what I made it for. I have the same with spells where I just want to try them out or watch a video to see what they do. When I build characters I always look up a guide if it is not provided in the game. So, I usually don't really think about it in this much detail. I liked it though. I thought it was pleasant to do and was curious how good the party that I was creating would be in the game.
answers.optional_comments: My interest in Baldur's Gate 3 increased while making my characters mainly because I was curious about what these characters would be like in game.
answers.first_change: I would add more explanations in the form of tooltips especially for the ability scores. I had to search up a lot of definitions and what would be good for my character. So, I would have liked it if the tool explained to me what Wisdom, Intelligence, Charisma, History, Athletics, Perception, etc. would change to my character.
